title: Amauropelta gracilis
intro: Amauropelta gracilis, also known as the slender maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to South America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and slender stems. The health benefits of Amauropelta gracilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta gracilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Amauropelta gracilis is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Amauropelta gracilis is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae and order Polypodiales. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta. This classification places Amauropelta gracilis among the many species of ferns found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas.
